title: MaxSortedTopRows has no effect
description:
Using Virtuoso compiled from the latest develop/7 commit (cfb50e3), setting MaxSortedTopRows in virtuoso.ini doesn't have any effect. When I set MaxSortedTopRows to 40000, restart Virtuoso, and execute the following query:
SELECT *
WHERE {
  ?s ?p ?o .
}
ORDER BY ?s
LIMIT 1000
OFFSET 10000

I see this error: Virtuoso 22023 Error SR353: Sorted TOP clause specifies more then 11000 rows to sort. Only 10000 are allowed. Either decrease the offset and/or row count or use a scrollable cursor
Is MaxSortedTopRows still supported?
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (1, 'Test A', 123.45);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, value) VALUES (2, 'Test B', NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table ORDER BY id LIMIT 5000 OFFSET 2000;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT * FROM test_table ORDER BY id LIMIT 1000 OFFSET 5000) AS subquery ORDER BY id;
key_bug_features: MaxSortedTopRows setting not applied in query execution
